introSection: Black Widow was a steel launched shuttle roller coaster built by American firm Arrow Development that previously operated at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened on May 14, 1977 and was called Loop Coaster. Its name was later changed to Black Widow. In 1999, the ride was closed and removed to make way for Flashback, a Vekoma Boomerang relocated from Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom that opened the following year later in 2000, coinciding with the rebranding of the park to Six Flags New England.

The ride was then sent to the former site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in 2000 with other former rides Little Rickies Little Twister and Rotor, where Six Flags had planned to build an amusement park. The park was never built and the Black Widow was eventually scrapped by 2003 after the land it sat on was sold in July 2002.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content: The Black Widow is a highly venomous spider.

sections.title: External links
sections.content:

fullText:
Black Widow was a steel launched shuttle roller coaster built by American firm Arrow Development that previously operated at Riverside Park in Agawam, Massachusetts, USA.

The ride opened on May 14, 1977 and was called Loop Coaster. Its name was later changed to Black Widow. In 1999, the ride was closed and removed to make way for Flashback, a Vekoma Boomerang relocated from Six Flags Kentucky Kingdom that opened the following year later in 2000, coinciding with the rebranding of the park to Six Flags New England.

The ride was then sent to the former site of Old Indiana Fun-n-Water Park in 2000 with other former rides Little Rickies Little Twister and Rotor, where Six Flags had planned to build an amusement park. The park was never built and the Black Widow was eventually scrapped by 2003 after the land it sat on was sold in July 2002.



Bordeux track and black supports.



The Black Widow is a highly venomous spider.
